title: Hemerocallis cordifolia
taxonomy: Hemerocallis cordifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It is native to eastern Asia and is a type of daylily. The genus Hemerocallis is a group of plants that are known for their showy flowers, which bloom for a single day. The plant is often used in landscaping due to its hardy and adaptable nature. Hemerocallis cordifolia is a perennial that thrives in a variety of environments.
uses: Hemerocallis cordifolia, also known as the heart-leaved daylily, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, it is believed to have antioxidant and antiviral properties, which may help protect against infections and oxidative stress. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Hemerocallis cordifolia.

intro: Hemerocallis cordifolia, also known as Day Lily or Tien Chi Huang, is a perennial plant native to East Asia, belonging to the lily family (Asphodelaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemerocallis cordifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies such as cough syrups and respiratory tonics.
